supply_chain.dependencies: The nicotine used in our operating companies' innovative smoke-free products is extracted from tobacco produced in one country. Single-source supplier risk for nicotine extract. Concentration risk in China for e-vapor product manufacturing (NJOY contracts with third-party importers). Wood tips used in cigar products and aluminum used in packaging subject to supply chain disruptions. Tobacco leaf varieties only available in limited geographies pose additional risks to price, availability and quality.
supply_chain.risks: Shifts in crops driven by macroeconomic conditions and adverse weather patterns. Government restrictions and mandated prices. Production control programs. Economic trade sanctions. Import duties and tariffs. International trade disruptions. Labor disruptions. Inflation. Geopolitical instability. Climate and environmental changes. Disruptions due to man-made or natural disasters. Reduced demand for tobacco leaf may result in reduced supply and availability of domestic tobacco and increased costs. Macroeconomic factors such as tariffs may exacerbate reductions in demand for tobacco leaf. Unavailability of nicotine extract necessary to manufacture products. Current geopolitical and macroeconomic conditions causing worldwide disruptions and delays to supply chains. Limited access to and increased cost of raw materials, ingredients and component parts. Reduced demand for raw materials may reduce supply and availability. Financial or operational difficulties of suppliers, distributors, logistics providers or third-party partners. Government taxes, restrictions and prohibitions on sale and use of certain products. Single-use plastic bans and extended producer responsibility mandates. Reliance on a few significant facilities and a small number of key suppliers, distributors and distribution chain service providers. Natural or man-made disaster, cybersecurity incident, global pandemic, labor disruption or other disruption affecting manufacturing operations or key suppliers.

regulatory_risks: FDA regulatory actions and inaction under the Family Smoking Prevention and Tobacco Control Act (FSPTCA)
FDA pre-market review pathways (PMTA, Substantial Equivalence) and enforcement policy
FDA marketing denial orders (MDOs) for e-vapor products
FDA potential product standards (nicotine reduction in cigarettes, menthol ban, flavor bans in cigars and e-vapor products, NNN levels in smokeless tobacco)
FDA tobacco product manufacturing practices proposed rule
FDA user fees imposed on cigarette, smokeless tobacco, cigar and pipe tobacco manufacturers
FDA graphic warnings for cigarettes
FDA underage access and use enforcement actions
State and local legislation imposing restrictions on tobacco products (flavor bans, sale restrictions, characterizing flavor bans, nicotine caps)
Federal, state and local legislation increasing legal age to purchase tobacco products to 21
Excise tax increases at federal, state and local levels
State extended producer responsibility legislation concerning packaging
Single-use plastic bans and limitations
World Health Organization Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (FCTC)
State Settlement Agreements (MSA) payment obligations and NPM Adjustment disputes
Smokeless Tobacco Master Settlement Agreement (STMSA) marketing and advertising restrictions
Federal Cigarette Labeling and Advertising Act preemption issues
Racketeer Influenced and Corrupt Organizations Act (RICO) claims
Federal Government's lawsuit RICO injunctions and corrective statements
Patent infringement claims and ITC exclusion orders (NJOY ACE, IQOS System)
Antitrust claims related to JUUL investment
Environmental laws and regulations (Clean Air Act, Clean Water Act, Resource Conservation and Recovery Act, Superfund)
Foreign privacy laws and data protection regulations
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and anti-bribery laws
Corporate alternative minimum tax (Corporate AMT) under Inflation Reduction Act
1% excise tax on share repurchases under Inflation Reduction Act
State and local government health and safety requirements during pandemics
Canadian tobacco litigation and Companies' Creditors Arrangement Act proceedings
International trade sanctions and export controls
Compliance with FDA requirements for e-vapor product registries in select states

sanction_risks: Economic trade sanctions impacting tobacco supply
International armed conflicts causing supply chain disruptions
Geopolitical instability affecting tobacco leaf availability and quality
Government prohibitions on import or export of tobacco in certain countries
Tariffs increasing cost of purchasing tobacco leaf from suppliers in other countries
